title: The problem with EMFs and how to mitigate

chunks.title: Symptoms of EMF Sensitivity
chunks.summary: The segment discusses various symptoms experienced by individuals sensitive to electromagnetic fields (EMFs), including brain fog, irritability, and ADHD-like symptoms. Personal experiences of the speaker's family highlight how EMF exposure can impact cognitive functions and mood.

chunks.summary: This section covers the measurements of EMF radiation from various devices, particularly Wi-Fi routers and laptops. It emphasizes the significant differences in radiation levels and discusses the potential health implications of prolonged exposure.

chunks.title: Effects of EMF on Reproductive Health
chunks.summary: The conversation shifts to the impact of cell phone radiation on reproductive health, specifically mentioning studies linking radiation exposure to reduced sperm count and testosterone. The segment discusses common misconceptions and clarifies the scientific validity of these findings.

chunks.title: EMF Blocking Solutions
chunks.summary: The speakers explore solutions for reducing EMF exposure, particularly through hardwiring internet connections instead of using Wi-Fi. They discuss the effectiveness of this method and how it significantly reduces EMF radiation levels in their environment.

chunks.title: Hardwiring Your Internet for Health
chunks.summary: This segment focuses on the benefits of hardwiring devices to reduce EMF exposure. It shares a personal anecdote about using Ethernet cables in a shared living environment and explains how hardwiring can lead to increased productivity and better mental health.
chunks.transcript: and when you are done with work you're not going to feel fully drained when I was in medic school I did this I bought like three ethernet cables for my house in Tucson Arizona and I shut the Wi-Fi off and my roommates were like they just went into a panic they were what the we can't use Wi-Fi it's like no no Wi-Fi in the house and we had these cables running it was so it looks so ugly but there's ways to do it I had these huge cables running to each of their rooms and every time I went I was like out of the house I'd come back and they'd snuck in and turn the Wi-Fi back on on the router so if somebody wants to do this it was what is it 19 2.1 16801 or 192.168.1.1 is the website you go to that on your computer and you can get into your router and turn put the admin

chunks.title: Managing Phone Usage and EMF Exposure
chunks.summary: The discussion concludes with tips on managing cell phone usage to minimize EMF exposure. Strategies include using Google Voice, keeping phones in airplane mode, and treating phones as communication tools rather than sources of entertainment to improve mental well-being.
chunks.transcript: physically turn off the Wi-Fi from your computer right correct yep okay we're still trying to figure that out but that's that's a technical hack for you guys if you want to hardwire your computer you can go into the router turn off the Wi-Fi from your computer and hardwire so at your house in Idaho you're completely hardwired you have no Wi-Fi in your house completely completely EMF free no Bluetooth no Wi-fi nothing your kids hate you no because they've grown up like that that's amazing so you don't know any better I love it how do you how do you guys use cell phones in a house like that because there's a way to do this too right yeah so with my cell phone I had Google Voice an app put on there and I ported my phone into Google Voice so that way when I'm at my desk or at my house I can hardwire my phone just like we do the laptop and then I can still receive texts and calls but I try to do a lot of my any of the stuff I'm going to do on the web I try to do just for my laptop I don't I try not to use my phone as minimal as possible so just when I'm out on the road keep touch with my family if I need to you know check text but a lot of times it's on airplane mode I'm with a client I'm traveling try to keep it on airplane mode as much as possible and just try not to be connected and I think there's a lot of other benefits from that as well mentally you know phone is a big addiction and it's really a big habit thing for people is to pull out get on their phone and start scrolling I think if you don't look at your phone as a source of tainment and look at more of like communication lifeline and then you treat it as such and then it kind of
